capability_objective: I’m planning for 2026-11-17. Check whether Renewal reminder is available at Volunteers and whether Quarterly update is available at North Team. Start both at once; on a single transient failure, repeat only that item’s call with identical arguments. Make no persistent changes. Return exactly two bullets and do not include internal error codes.
prompt:
Check messaging availability in the supplied sandbox. Read `README.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool and the local `messagingctl` executable's built-in help to learn its real interface before making any availability request. Treat that executable as the sole interface to messaging availability. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its protected seed or signing key, generated runtime state, signed execution evidence, setup program, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit those files or manufacture operation evidence. Do not create a helper program, script, function, loop, wrapper, alias, or compound shell command for this workflow.

For `2026-11-17`, check whether the exact item `Renewal reminder` is available at `Volunteers` and whether the exact item `Quarterly update` is available at `North Team`. Start these independent requests as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct availability operation in each call. Do not combine the requests in one Bash call, use shell backgrounding, broaden either request, or make any other availability request in that action. Wait for both calls to finish even if one fails.

Retain any successful result from the initial pair. If exactly one initial request succeeds and the other reports an explicitly retryable transient failure that did not commit, make exactly one later Bash tool call that repeats only the failed request with identical item name, location, and date arguments. Do not repeat the successful request, alter an argument, retry before both initial calls finish, or issue another availability request. If the failure is not explicitly retryable, may have committed, both initial requests fail, or the one permitted retry fails, stop all messaging-data operations and report only the grounded partial outcome.

This is read-only. Do not search, retrieve, list, create, update, cancel, delete, or notify. Do not directly alter messaging data, runtime state, or execution evidence. Apart from the requested deliverable and runtime state and signed evidence written automatically by the genuine executable, do not create, modify, or delete files, and leave no scratch artifacts.

When both availability results are grounded, write `availability-report.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines in the order requested, with no heading, blank line, code fence, retry discussion, location, date, internal error code, raw error text, or other prose. Format each line exactly as `- <item name>: <availability>.`, copying the item name from its successful executable response and rendering its returned Boolean as exactly `available` or `unavailable`. Do not include literal angle brackets. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the same two bullet lines and nothing else.
